raw_consumer_statement:
XX/XX/XXXX - XXXX reached out to me about booking a hotel for a conference in XXXX from XXXX, XXXX. I believed that this company was working on behalf of the publishing company that would be paying for my trip. I agreed to book a hotel and then had a brief email exchange about which hotel I wanted. I confirmed that on the phone with " elite booking services ''. 

XX/XX/XXXX- I emailed to cancel the reservation when we realized that this company was not, in fact, representing her publisher. 

XX/XX/XXXX - I didn't hear back from " XXXX XXXX '' and discovered that this company has reportedly scammed customers in the past, so I initiated a dispute with Citibank. 

XX/XX/XXXX- Citibank resolved the dispute in the company 's favor. 

XX/XX/XXXX- I learned in a phone conversation with XXXX XXXX of elitebooking.com that an email had been sent to me about the cancellation policy. I then found it in my spam folder. 

XX/XX/XXXX- I again confirmed by email that we would like to cancel the reservation. 

XX/XX/XXXX- I emailed again to confirm the cancellation and acknowledge the 25 % cancellation fee. 

XX/XX/XXXX- I contacted Citibank to reopen the dispute. 

XX/XX/XXXX- XXXX XXXX  ( XXXX XXXX ) finally answered my call and acknowledged that we had cancelled the reservation and would have her assistant process the refund. There has been no refund and no response from them through email or telephone. 

XX/XX/XXXX - I spoke with the hotel where the reservation was supposed to be made and confirmed that there was NOT one made for us by XXXX XXXX XXXX. 

XXXX XXXX - XXXX received a letter from Citibank saying that they have not received the requested " credit slip '' and have closed our dispute. I sent every bit of evidence I have of the transaction ( including what I believe to be a " credit slip '' ) and can't understand what could be missing. On the Citibank website it still has our disputed listed as " pending ''.
